other_observers: Cole Wild, Sol Miller, Sue Riffer, Bob Smith, Andrew Spencer and about 5 others (I don't know their names); I am the original finder and identifier
specific_location: North shore of Fossil Creek Reservoir
description: Small goose, about the size of "Richardson's" Cackling Goose, with all black head, neck, breast; one one side of neck (right side I believe) a faint hint of a white crescent shaped mark was visible near the throat; small black bill; dark legs; pale, mottled brown sides, bright white lower belly and vent (seen from the side); dark gray-brown back and wings with thin white terminal bars on the secondary coverts of the wings noticeable upon studying the photo images.Behaviors: Walking on shoreline and swimming in shallow water.Call: not heardPlumage: juvenal or subadult
